title: alyssum petraeum subsp. microcarpon
intro: Alyssum petraeum subsp. microcarpon, also known as small-fruited alpine alyssum, is a flowering plant species within the Alyssum genus. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Alyssum petraeum subsp. microcarpon can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms of traditional remedies.
